text.title: Laravel Queues Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Laravel queue setups.

antipattern.symptom: Job processes 2-day-old data; user is surprised to receive email referring to cancelled order.
antipattern.root-cause: Job constructor stored the Order model; payload serialized at dispatch time; processed later from stale snapshot.
antipattern.fix: Pass `$orderId` (int) in constructor; reload `Order::findOrFail($this->orderId)` inside handle().

antipattern.symptom: Transient gateway timeout retries the charge job → customer charged twice.
antipattern.root-cause: Charge job not idempotent; retried payload triggers second charge.
antipattern.fix: Job stores idempotency_key (UUID per logical request); inside handle(), check whether charge with that key exists; skip if present.

antipattern.symptom: One slow job hangs a worker indefinitely; queue depth grows; SLA breached.
antipattern.root-cause: Job has no $timeout; worker waits forever.
antipattern.fix: Set `$timeout = 30` (or appropriate); Horizon kills worker process if exceeded; job retried per $tries.

antipattern.symptom: Operator unaware of 200 failed jobs in queue:failed_jobs table; business actions silently dropped.
antipattern.root-cause: Job has no failed() method; failure goes only to default log.
antipattern.fix: Add `public function failed(Throwable $e) { Sentry::captureException($e); Notification::route('slack', '...')->notify(new JobFailed($this, $e)); }`.
